node.title: Google | Data structure to set all values in O(1)

topic.title: Google | Data structure to set all values in O(1)

post.content: No LeetCode version of this question??
Just linking this here as it seems related https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-question/124621/Data-Structure-get(int-idx)-set(int-idx-int-val)-and-setAll(int-val)-all-in-constant-time./

[@Sithis](https://leetcode.com/Sithis) \n\nYou have the right idea but the execution is wrong. Instead of using System.nanoTime() which is indeterministic, just have your own epoch, starting at 0. If setAll is called, increment the epoch. Then, check if it was last updated during this epoch. If not, return the last value for setAll.

One thing to think about for folks who solve this by replacing a data structure on `setAll`: Although creating a new data structure is `O(1)`, you sign up the garbage collector for having to free up `O(n)` space when you break your reference to the old collection. In C(++) where you have to manage memory yourself this is more apparent, but it is less apparent in garbage collected languages since that `O(n)` step is handled behind the scenes. \n\nI think different interviewers may have different opinions as to whether or not this counts as `O(1)`, so I recommend clarifying with them but also suggesting that a collection replacement should be `O(n)`.
huff..so easy. I blew it up in my onsite.
i came across this question in another company\'s interview.
